title: Verify Cerebras is actually serving normie traffic (formalize the 30-sec sanity check from S-053)
current_state: S-053 wired Cerebras as normie primary with Groq fallback. Code path: pi_agent.py:140-150 instantiates self.cerebras as an OpenAI client; _respond_normie tries Cerebras first then Groq. The only test that ran was a syntactic import smoke test ('pi_agent imports clean'). No interactive test has confirmed Cerebras actually responds to a normie turn â€” if the OpenAI-client response shape differs from Groq's, or there's a subtle wire-up bug, the first real normie conversation will silently fall back to Groq with no error surfaced, or fail entirely.
structural_problem: Wire-up bugs hide under syntactic green. The build looks fine but the runtime path was never exercised. Pattern: anything we'd discover 'the next time we use it' is a missing test.
target_state: scripts/sanity_check_normie.py opens a normie turn, asserts response came from Cerebras (not Groq), confirms response shape parses correctly, confirms turn lands in logs/turns.jsonl + L1. Can be run in 10 seconds anytime. A pytest equivalent runs in CI via verify.py.
files_affected: tests/test_normie_provider.py (new â€” pytest version for verify.py)
evolution.py / pi_agent.py (ensure used_provider field is logged per S-053 plan â€” verify it actually is)
success_criteria: Running `python scripts/sanity_check_normie.py` in <10s outputs: provider=cerebras, response_length>0, l1_row_written=True, evolution_logged=True
Script fails loudly if Cerebras is reachable but normie still routes to Groq (the wire-up bug case)
Script tolerates Cerebras being down â€” falls back to Groq AND reports 'fallback used: cerebras_unreachable: <reason>'
tests/test_normie_provider.py mocks the Cerebras client and asserts _respond_normie picks it up first
Manually: open Pi, type 'hey', confirm via daemon stats or evolution.jsonl that used_provider=cerebras (one-time sanity)
risk_notes: Discovery ticket. Worst case: this surfaces an actual S-053 wire-up bug, in which case open a new ticket to fix and link it.
migration_plan: 1. Run the manual 30-sec sanity check FIRST â€” open Pi, switch to normie if not already, say 'hey', then `python pi.py --stats` or read latest evolution.jsonl row, confirm used_provider field
2. If used_provider is missing or wrong: open a follow-up ticket; pause this one until fixed

3. If sanity passes: write scripts/sanity_check_normie.py that does the same in code â€” instantiate PiAgent, call process_input('hey') in normie mode, read back the evolution.jsonl row, assert used_provider=cerebras
4. Write tests/test_normie_provider.py â€” mock both providers, assert dispatch order
5. Add scripts/sanity_check_normie.py invocation to verify.py (so CI catches regression)
resolution: DISCOVERY: CEREBRAS_API_KEY is blank in .env — normie has been running on Groq (tier=cheap fallback) all along. Groq confirmed working. Cerebras key pending setup by Ash. sanity_check_normie.py + test_normie_provider.py (5 tests) shipped to catch regression once key is added.
